---
format: html
title: append_missing_taxa_to_template()
---

<html><head>
<meta http-equiv="Content-Type" content="text/html; charset=utf-8" />
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, user-scalable=yes" />
<link rel="stylesheet" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.css">
<script type="text/javascript">
const macros = { "\\R": "\\textsf{R}", "\\mbox": "\\text", "\\code": "\\texttt"};
function processMathHTML() {
    var l = document.getElementsByClassName('reqn');
    for (let e of l) { katex.render(e.textContent, e, { throwOnError: false, macros }); }
    return;
}</script>
<script defer src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.15.3/dist/katex.min.js"
    onload="processMathHTML();"></script>
<link rel="stylesheet" type="text/css" href="R.css" />
</head><body><div class="container"><main>

<table style="width: 100%;"><tr><td>append_missing_taxa_to_template</td><td style="text-align: right;">R Documentation</td></tr></table>

<h2>Append missing taxa to the shared template CSV</h2>

<h3>Description</h3>

<p>Reads the existing missing-taxa template CSV (if present),
removes any entries that are now present in
'data_classification_table' (i.e. have since been classified),
appends any new unclassified taxa, deduplicates by 'sel_name',
and writes the result back to disk. If the file does not yet
exist it is created. Nothing is written when 'data_missing_taxa'
is empty, the file already exists, and no stale entries need to
be removed.
</p>


<h3>Usage</h3>

<pre><code class='language-R'>append_missing_taxa_to_template(
  data_missing_taxa,
  file_path = here::here("Data/Input/missing_taxa_template.csv"),
  data_classification_table = NULL
)
</code></pre>


<h3>Arguments</h3>

<table role = "presentation">
<tr><td><code id="data_missing_taxa">data_missing_taxa</code></td>
<td>
<p>A tibble with columns 'sel_name', 'kingdom', 'phylum', 'class',
'order', 'family', 'genus', and 'species' (all character).
Rows represent taxa that could not be classified. Pass an empty
tibble (zero rows) when there are no new missing taxa.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="file_path">file_path</code></td>
<td>
<p>A length-1 character string giving the path to the CSV file.
Defaults to
'here::here(&quot;Data/Input/missing_taxa_template.csv&quot;)'.</p>
</td></tr>
<tr><td><code id="data_classification_table">data_classification_table</code></td>
<td>
<p>'NULL' (default) or a data frame with at least a 'sel_name'
column. When provided, any entry already present in the
existing template whose 'sel_name' also appears in this table
is removed from the template before merging. This prevents
stale entries from re-appearing after a taxon has been manually
added to the auxiliary classification table.</p>
</td></tr>
</table>


<h3>Details</h3>

<p>The file is only written when at least one of the following is
true:
</p>

<ul>
<li><p> 'data_missing_taxa' contains one or more rows.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> The file at 'file_path' does not yet exist.
</p>
</li>
<li><p> 'data_classification_table' is not 'NULL' and at least
one existing template entry is now classified.
</p>
</li></ul>

<p>Deduplication keeps the first occurrence of each 'sel_name'.
</p>


<h3>Value</h3>

<p>The 'file_path' string (invisibly), so it can be used as a
'format = &quot;file&quot;' target return value in a targets pipeline.
</p>


<h3>See Also</h3>

<p>[get_aux_classification_table()],
[get_taxa_without_classification()],
[check_and_report_missing_taxa()]
</p>

</main>

</div>
</body></html>
